titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Photographs of the Manzanar War Relocation Center, California
Photographs of the Manzanar War Relocation Center, California
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 9, 2021 at 04:01

did.unittitle: Photographs of the Manzanar War Relocation Center, California

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Inscriptions and ink stamps in English.

did.abstract: Black and white photographs of the Japanese American relocation camp in Manzanar, California photographed in 1943 by Ansel Adams and printed from the original negatives by Alan Ross in 1985 for the Fresno Metropolitan Museum of Art, History, and Science in Fresno, California.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Black and white photographs of the Japanese American relocation camp in Manzanar, California photographed in 1943 by Ansel Adams and printed from the original negatives by Alan Ross in 1985 for the Fresno Metropolitan Museum of Art, History, and Science in Fresno, California.
The photographs concentrate on the internees and their activities, including family life in the barracks, people at work, and recreational activities.

Ink stamps on versos: "Photograph by Ansel Adams, Born Free and Equal, Photographs of the Manzanar War Relocation Center, 1943-1944. Museum print from the original negative by Alan Ross, Fall, 1985. Produced in Cooperation with the Fresno Metropolitan Museum and the Library of Congress."
Signed, titled, and numbered by Alan Ross on versos.
Inscriptions on versos: FMM Set #4.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Janet Sirmon Fine Art on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2016.

bioghist.head: Ansel Easton Adams (1902-1984)
bioghist.p: Ansel Easton Adams was an American photographer and environmentalist. In fall 1943, Adams captured images at the Manzanar War Relocation Center in Inyo County, California, at the eastern edge of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

bioghist.head: Alan Ross (born 1932)
bioghist.p: Alan Ross is a photographer and educator. He was a photographic assistant to Ansel Adams from 1974 to 1984. In 1984-1985, a traveling exhibition of original photographic prints from the Ansel Adams's Photographs of Japanese-American Internment at Manzanar collection of the Library of Congress traveled to Fresno, California; Denver, Colorado; and Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. In 1985, Ross created sets of modern prints from the original negatives held by the Library of Congress, which traveled to more venues. Emily Medvec curated the traveling exhibitions and wrote an introduction to the catalog for them: Born Free and Equal: An Exhibition of Ansel Adams Photographs (Washington, D.C.: Fresno Metropolitan Museum of Art, History, and Science by Echolight Corporation, 1984).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ansel Adams, Photographs of the Manzanar War Relocation Center, California. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Titles for prints in this guide derive from the titles given by Adams to original negatives held at the Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division, and the descriptions derive from image descriptions by that institution.

unitdatestructured.altrender: images created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Birds sit on power lines above buildings, mountains and setting sun in the background.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wooden sign at entrance to the Manzanar War Relocation Center with a car at the gatehouse in the background.

title.part: Manzanar from Guard Tower, View West (Sierra Nevada in Background)

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bird's-eye view of center grounds showing buildings, roads and mountains in background.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yuichi Harata (1915-1982), bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of young people, mostly women, holding books, walking on a dirt road, buildings in the distance.

title.part: Mrs. Yaeko Nakamura and her Two Children, Joyce Yuki (right) and Louise Tami (left), Standing on the Step at the Entrance of a Dwelling

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Yaeko Kusayanagi Nakamura (1909-2008) and her daughters, Tamiko Louise Nakamura (born 1938), and Yukiko Joyce Nakamura (born 1934).

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tōyō Miyatake (1895-1979), full-length portrait, standing in his children's bedroom looking at his young daughter, Minnie Mineko Miyatake (born 1938), seated at a desk, drawing, while her mother, Hiro Miyatake (1904-1972), stands behind her. Inscription on verso misidentifies the image as the Mitsu Nakai family.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tamiko Louise Nakamura (born 1938), youngest daughter in Nakamura family, bust portrait, facing front. Print lacks an ink stamp and pencil inscriptions on verso.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japanese-style garden with pool at dusk with mountains in the background and moon.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spectators of all ages, seated and standing, watch a band perform in the afternoon on an outdoor stage.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tōyō Miyatake (1895-1979), bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nobutaro Harry Sumida (1871-1950), full-length portrait, seated, facing left, reading a book, next to a small table. "Oldest Neisi in Center," according to caption on the negative sleeve.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kenji Sano (1925-1989), farmer second generation, bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notices of available employment posted on an outdoor bulletin board.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of people standing, facing right, near the open door of an automobile, buildings in the background.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two way traffic in the camp, and camp buildings.

title.part: Mr. and Mrs. Henry J. Tsurutani and Baby Bruce

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Henry Junya Tsurutani (1908-2003), Aya Lucy Imai Tsurutani (1913-2013), and Bruce Tadashi Tsurutani (born 1941) at table with shelving units along walls. Inscription on verso misidentifies the image as the Tōyō Miyatake family.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japanese Americans observe an amateur baseball game in progress; one-story buildings and mountains in the background.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yukiko Joyce Nakamura (born 1934), bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: People of all ages wait in a line in front of a building at midday.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hidimi Art Tayenaka (1915-1996), standing, facing left, pushing a piece of wood through the planer.

title.part: Roy Takeno, Town Hall Meeting from Side

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy Makoto Takeno (1913-1997), standing, addresses a group of men gathered for a meeting.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Close-up of tractor with Benji Iguchi (1915-1992) pulling a rope.

title.part: Two Young men in Co-Op Goods Store, Satoru Nasunoko & Bill Idama

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japanese American man and Kiyonobu Bill Idama (1920-2009) seated on crates of bottles in the storeroom, both smoke cigarettes. According to the records of the United States War Relocation Authority, there was no one with the name "Satoru Nasunoko" at Manzanar War Relocation Center or other relocation center.

title.part: Michael Yonemitsu X-Ray Technician, and Harry Sumida in X-Ray Room

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Michael Koichi Yonemitsu (1914-2010) holds the right hand of Nobutaro Harry Sumida (1871-1950) on an x-ray plate.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two babies stand in one crib, one baby lays down with head up in another crib.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three girls standing against wall.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: United States Army Corporal James Hatsuto Shohara (1920-1993), head-and-shoulders portrait, facing front, in uniform.

title.part: Pictures and Mementoes on Phonograph Top: Yonemitsu Home

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autographed photograph of United States Army Private Robert Takeo Yonemitsu (1921-2002), religious card, stamped envelopes, ornamental squash and a potted plant on doily on a table top.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aiko Hamaguchi Morita (1918-2013), bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tamekichi Carl Hibino (1880-1953), artist, three-quarter length portrait, standing, facing front, holding paintbrush and palette, in front of large painting.

title.part: Roy Takeno, Editor, and Group Reading Paper in Front of Office

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roy Makoto Takeno (1913-1997) (far left), with Nabou Sawamura (1921-1988) and Yuichi Hirata (1915-1982) , standing in front of the Office of Reports Free Press. Two men are reading the Los Angeles Times newspaper.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yonehisa Yamagami (1917-2001), bust portrait, facing slightly right, a cigarette holder between his teeth.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Students seated in a classroom laboratory watching instructor at front of room.

title.part: Mr. Matsumoto and Children at Children's Garden

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harry Haruo Matsumoto (1908-1988) on wooden steps with nine children from the "Shonien," Japanese for Children's Home, also known as Children's Garden and Children's Village, an orphanage for children at the Manzanar War Relocation Center.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yoshio Muramoto (1920-1985), bust portrait, facing front.

title.part: Warehouse, M. Ogi, Manager; S. Sugimoto, Manager of Co-Op; and Bunkichi Hayashi

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monji Ogi (1903-1978), Sokichi Sugimoto (1894-1977) manager of Cooperative Enterprises, and Bunkichi Hayashi (1915-1972) standing among shelves with boxes in warehouse.

title.part: Farm, Farm Workers, Mount Williamson in Background

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farm workers harvesting crops in field, mountains in the background. Inscription on verso references another image, Potato fields .

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four boys seated in a Sunday school class.

title.part: Japanese-American United States Naval Cadet Nurse, Kay Fukuda

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kikuko Kay Hikuko Fukuda Ochiai (1919-1998), three-quarter length portrait, standing, facing front, in military uniform.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Moritaka Nakashima (1919-1993), full-length portrait, standing, facing front, scattering chicken feed from a pail in front of a chicken coop.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sumiko Shigematsu Murakami (1921-2013), half-length portrait, seated at textile machinery, facing right.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: People walking through relocation center in snow past hand made wood fence.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tsuna Higashi Nakaji (1892-1979), also known as Otsuno Nakaji, three-quarter length portrait, standing against the open door of an automobile, facing left.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kishio Matoba (1921-2000), bust portrait, facing front.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of men and boys standing around a small brush fire holding shovels, a pitchfork stands in the right foreground, mountains in background.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Visitors of all ages view an exhibit of Ansel Adams' photographs of Manzanar and sculpture displayed on walls and tables.

title.part: Loading Bus, Leaving Manzanar for Relocation

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Man with pipe stands on top of bus loading luggage into rack, while others gather around.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marble monument with Kanji inscription that reads, "Monument for the Pacification of Spirits," with mountains in the background, including Mount Williamson.

unitdatestructured.altrender: image created in 1943 and printed in 1985
unitdatestructured.label: creation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Young girl, half-length portrait, standing, facing left.
